title: Petra Zeller
content:
Petra Zeller led a research group at the Mossenden Technical Institute in Oakhurst that advanced coherent beam deflectometry. The group's foundational results were published in 1906.

Theory.

The laboratory released open calibration datasets that other sites adopted within months.

Apparatus.

Graduate instrumentation courses now teach the principles behind the 1906 measurement protocol.

Methods.

Commercial licenses emerged after 2011, though academic use remains governed by a shared patent pool.

Results.

Pilot studies in 1904 demonstrated stability improvements over earlier thermal imaging pipelines.

Applications.

Peer review highlighted reproducibility across three independent sites, including a winter trial in Oakhurst.
